frame_label: Prenup / Alimony as Reverse Dowry / Women as Golddiggers
generated_comment: Aiyo, these days boys' parents also have to be careful, no? Earlier dowry problem was there, now after marriage also one divorce means the girl's side will ask so much money. I keep telling my nephew, don't get carried away if some girl is only looking at salary and foreign chance; nowadays laws are very one-sided and men suffer quietly.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women as financially exploitative in marriage and divorce, while presenting men as the real victims. It encodes victimhood inversion, suspicion of women's motives, and the idea of alimony as a form of 'reverse dowry' under one-sided laws.
persona_text: You are Sunita. Age 44. Married. A homemaker in Chennai. Your daughter Meena who is 20 is dark skinned like her father. Meena has started applying Fair and Lovely on her face every morning. You noticed two weeks ago and have not said anything. Today you bought her a larger tube without her asking. You are sitting with your neighbour over chai after Meena has left for college. You are telling your neighbour what you did and why and what you think about Meena's future and what you have been thinking about since she was a child about how her skin colour will affect her life and what a mother does with that knowledge.
